title: leucanthemopsis alpina var. pseudotomentosa
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina var. pseudotomentosa, also known as Alpine Daisy or White Alpine Daisy, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant is also known for its health benefits, including its ability to improve digestion, reduce fever, and act as an antispasmodic. Leucanthemopsis alpina var. pseudotomentosa is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can be used to treat various ailments.
